subject: Re: Issues with denoising
body:
Hi Julien,

I have it running on both 10 and 11, so that shouldn't be a problem. Visual 
Studio isn't needed, but the VC++ redistributable 
is: https://aka.ms/vs/17/release/vc_redist.x64.exe

If you try to run a command line tool like Noise2Map 
<http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=389>, does it say anything useful?

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Wednesday, October 26, 2022 at 7:01:09 AM UTC-7 julien....@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hello Dimitry,
>
> We installed the latest GPU driver has you suggested to us and there is no 
> more error about CUDA but now it claims that the DLL file was not found.
> The windows version has just been upgraded from 10 to 11, can it be 
> related to our error?
> Also we have concerns about our Visual Studio version which is 2022 
> instead of 2017 as stated on WARP webpage. Is it a problem ?
>
> Thanks for your help,
> Cyan and Julien
>
>
>
> Le dimanche 9 octobre 2022 à 19:28:34 UTC+2, teg...@gmail.com a écrit :
>
>> Hi Cyan,
>>
>> Did you update the GPU drivers on that system when you installed 1.1.0? I 
>> think that's the most common issue so far. It might also be best to remove 
>> 1.0.9 just in case.
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>> On Thursday, October 6, 2022 at 2:03:21 AM UTC-7 xingyichen...@gmail.com 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Dear Dimitry,
>>>
>>> Thank you so much for the updates! 
>>>
>>> When using Warp 1.0.9, we had the following error in denoising:
>>>
>>> Unhandled Exception: System.Runtime.InteropServices.SEHException: 
>>> External component has thrown an exception at 
>>> Warp.GPU.Rotate3DExtractAt(UInt64 t_volume, int3 dimsvolume, IntPte d_proj, 
>>> int3 dimsproj, Single[] h_positions, UInt32 batch) at 
>>> Noise2Map.Program.Main(String[] args)
>>>
>>> GPU: Quadro RTX 6000
>>>
>>> Command: noise2map --observation1 even --observation2 odd --angpix 3.4 
>>> --batchsize 4 --dont_flatten_spectrum true --iterations 10000 
>>> --dont_augment true --gpuid_preprocess 0 --gpuid_network 0
>>>
>>> Now that we installed the beta version, we are starting to have the 
>>> following error:
>>>
>>> Unhandled exception. System.Exception: cudaErrorInvalidConfiguration
>>>    at Warp.GPU.CheckGPUExceptions()
>>>    at Noise2Map.Noise2Map.Main(String[] args)
>>>
>>> Do you maybe have suggestions on how to fix these issues?
>>>
>>> Thank you so much for your help!
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Cyan Ching
>>>
>>> PhD student
>>>
>>> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>>>
>>
